your_book:
Wednesday was a broadly red session for Jaleel's book, though the damage was contained — the portfolio shed just $18.74 in unrealized value (–0.09%) on a $21,594 account, with cash nearly exhausted at $68. The two bright spots were NVIDIA (+3.65%) and Broadcom (+4.83%), both catching a bid on AI-adjacent flows; AVGO's move was directly tied to news that Apple is committing over $30B to Broadcom for custom silicon and wireless components, a meaningful fundamental catalyst. NVDA's whale-alert session adds to its open watch flag around export controls and AI-capex sentiment. On the other side, NOW dropped –2.67% despite a Guggenheim note arguing AI concerns are overblown — ADBE, also flagged in that same note, only slipped –0.27%, so the market appears unconvinced on ServiceNow specifically. SPGI fell –2.88% and VRTX –4.56% with no headlines provided, making those moves harder to explain and worth watching closely.

The divergence-between-price-and-thesis list is getting longer. ADBE is now –9.9% in the book against a STRONG BUY / QARP 73.4 rating — the narrative overhang (Claude Design fear, CFO exit) continues to punish a stock that operationally beat and raised. RKLB sits at –15.5% with an AVOID verdict and a QARP of 39.2; at 7.3% of the portfolio it remains the single most glaring misfit for a capital-preservation mandate. LULU (–11.1%, elevated guidance-cut flag) and ZTS (–4.7%, –1.61% today) are both drifting further from thesis. BSX has no day price reported today, which warrants a data check ahead of its July 29 earnings.

Watches going forward: NOW reports July 22 and carries the largest single weight (14%) plus a high-multiple risk; any macro rate repricing or soft guidance would hit the book hard. ABT reports July 16 with a mild EPS flag already live. BSX's missing price data today is a loose end. And RKLB's continued drag demands a position-size rationale review — it is the one holding where price, verdict, and mandate are all pointing in the same direction: reduce.

attention.headline: –15.5% open loss, AVOID verdict, 7.3% weight — mandate misfit
attention.note: RKLB is the portfolio's worst performer, carries the only AVOID verdict, scores QARP 39.2, and sits at 7.3% of a capital-preservation book — all three signals align against holding it at this size.

attention.headline: –4.56% today with no headline — sharp move unexplained
attention.note: Vertex dropped nearly 5% on Wednesday with no provided catalyst; given its BUY verdict and 12.3% open gain, the session move is large enough to warrant identifying whether a pipeline or pricing-policy headline drove it.

attention.headline: No day price reported; earnings July 29 with guidance already slashed
attention.note: BSX is missing session price data today, and with FY26 growth guidance already cut to 7–8.5% and management guiding flat through Q3, any further softness at the July 29 print could accelerate the drift.

attention.headline: –2.88% today, now –3.6% in the book, no headline provided
attention.note: SPGI's near-3% single-session drop without an accompanying headline is worth flagging — at only 2% weight the dollar impact is small, but the move size relative to its normally low-volatility profile is unusual.
